vulnerabilities.title: jquery.terminal self XSS on user input
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact
This is low impact and limited XSS, because code for XSS payload is always visible, but attacker can use other techniques to hide the code the victim sees.

Also if the application use execHash option and execute code from URL the attacker can use this URL to execute his code. The scope is limited because the javascript code inside html attribute used is added to span tag, so no automatic execution like with `onerror` on images is possible.

### Patches
Fixed version 2.31.1

### Worka

vulnerabilities.title: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting in jquery.terminal
vulnerabilities.description:
Versions of `jquery.terminal` prior to 1.21.0 are vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting. If the application has either of the options `anyLinks` or `invokeMethods` set to true, the application may execute arbitrary JavaScript through crafted malicious payloads due to insufficient sanitization.


## Recommendation

Upgrade to version 1.21.0 or later
